title: Configure Tailwind, Vite, TypeScript & Vitest
description:
Set up all build/lint/test config files:
- tailwind.config.ts: content paths covering src/**
- postcss.config.cjs: tailwind + autoprefixer
- vite.config.ts: react plugin + vite-plugin-pwa stub (manifest TBD)
- tsconfig.json + tsconfig.node.json: strict mode, paths alias @ -> src/
- vitest.config.ts: jsdom environment, coverage thresholds
- src/styles/index.css: @tailwind directives only
- Update index.html: viewport meta, apple PWA meta tags, link to manifest

title: Initialise Vite + React + TypeScript project
description:
Run npm create vite with react-ts template, then install all required dependencies:
- react-router-dom v6
- idb v8
- nanoid
- vite-plugin-pwa
- tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer
- vitest, @vitest/coverage-v8, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/user-event, jsdom

Result: npm run dev starts a working dev server; npm run test runs (empty) Vitest suite.
close_reason: Vite React-TS scaffold in place, all deps installed, npm run test passes, npm run build succeeds

title: Epic: Project scaffold & tooling
description: Set up the Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS project from scratch. Covers package.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.json, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.cjs, vitest.config.ts, index.html, src/vite-env.d.ts, and src/styles/index.css.\n\nThis epic must be completed before any application code can be written.

title: Child home screen: profile picker
description: Update src/pages/child/home.tsx. Logic: if activeChild===null and profiles.length>1, render ProfilePicker component. If activeChild===null and profiles.length===1, auto-select the only profile (useEffect). If activeChild is set, render current home with greeting 'Hi {name}! Ready to practise?'. Create src/components/child/profile-picker.tsx: full-screen tablet-friendly grid of large name buttons (ProfilePicker({ profiles, onSelect })). Long-press parent-entry mechanic must remain on both the picker screen and the normal home screen.

title: Update use-attempts and use-revision-session hooks for child scoping
description: use-attempts.ts: consume useChild() from ChildContext. insertAttempt parameter becomes Omit<Attempt, 'childId'> — hook injects childId from activeChild. getWordStats filters by activeChild.id. use-revision-session.ts: pass activeChild.id to getAllAttempts so the revision algorithm only sees the current child's history. Guard: if activeChild is null, return empty word list (home screen prevents navigation to /session without a profile).

title: ChildContext and useChildProfile hook
description: Create src/context/child-context.tsx: ChildProvider + useChild() hook. Interface: { activeChild: ChildProfile | null, setActiveChild(profile), clearActiveChild() }. Persist active child id to localStorage key sp_ac. On mount, resolve stored id against live IDB profiles. Auto-clears if stored id no longer exists. Create src/hooks/use-child-profile.ts: useChildProfile() loads profiles list, exposes addProfile(name), renameProfile(id, name), removeProfile(id) with a guard blocking deletion if child has any attempts. Wire ChildProvider into src/main.tsx inside DbProvider and PinProvider.

title: DB access layer: profiles store and child-scoped attempt queries
description: Create src/db/profiles.ts with getAllProfiles, getProfile, upsertProfile, deleteProfile — mirror the pattern in src/db/weeks.ts. Update src/db/attempts.ts: all read functions (getAttemptsForWord, getAttemptsAfter, getAllAttempts, getRecentAttemptsGroupedByWord) gain a childId: string param and use the new by-childId index. Add getAttemptsByChild(db, childId) used by the profile-deletion guard. Export new functions from src/db/index.ts.

title: DB schema migration v1→v2: profiles store and childId index
description: Highest-risk step. Bump DB_VERSION to 2 in src/constants/db.ts and add STORE_PROFILES constant. Restructure upgrade() in src/db/schema.ts into versioned blocks. v2 migration must: (1) create profiles object store with by-name index, (2) add by-childId and by-childId-wordId indexes to attempts store, (3) create default ChildProfile { id: 'child-legacy-1', name: 'Child 1' } and stamp all existing attempts with childId='child-legacy-1' via cursor inside the upgrade transaction. Must write migration.test.ts BEFORE writing the migration code.

title: Add ChildProfile type and extend Attempt with childId
description: Foundation for multi-child support. Add ChildProfile interface { id, name, createdAt } to src/types/index.ts. Add required childId: string field to Attempt interface. Making childId non-optional means TypeScript immediately flags every broken call site, forcing a complete and correct implementation.

title: Multi-child profile support
description: Multiple children in a family need to share one device while keeping independent practice history. Add named child profiles so each child's attempt records are partitioned separately. Word lists remain shared (parent enters once). Includes DB migration from v1 (single-child) to v2 (multi-child), a profile picker on the home screen, per-child progress view, and profile management in Settings.

title: Implement use-revision-session hook
description:
src/hooks/use-revision-session.ts

On mount:
1. Fetch all weeks, all words, all attempts from IndexedDB (via use-db)
2. Determine lastSessionWordIds from the most recent batch of attempt records (same session = same date rounded to hour)
3. Pass all data as plain arrays to buildRevisionList() from src/lib/revisionList.ts
4. Store result in local state — never recompute mid-session

Returns: { words: Word[], currentIndex: number, advance(): void, isComplete: boolean, loading: boolean }

advance() increments currentIndex; when currentIndex reaches words.length, sets isComplete=true.

title: Implement use-tts hook
description:
src/hooks/use-tts.ts

API: { speak(text): void, cancel(): void, isSpeaking: boolean, isSupported: boolean }

Implementation details:
- isSupported = typeof window.speechSynthesis !== 'undefined'
- On mount: call getVoices(); listen to voiceschanged event (iOS loads voices async); select first en-GB voice, fallback en-AU, fallback any en-*, fallback default
- speak(): cancel any in-progress utterance first (prevents iOS queue buildup); create new SpeechSynthesisUtterance with rate=TTS_RATE; track isSpeaking via onstart/onend/onerror
- cancel(): calls speechSynthesis.cancel(); resets isSpeaking
- Must only be called in response to user gesture events — no autoplay on mount
- Cleanup: cancel on unmount

title: Implement data hooks (use-weeks, use-words, use-attempts)
description:
src/hooks/use-db.ts: exposes the IDBPDatabase from DbContext; throws if used outside provider
src/hooks/use-weeks.ts: loads all weeks, exposes upsertWeek and deleteWeek mutations
src/hooks/use-words.ts: loads words for a given weekId, exposes upsertWord and deleteWord
src/hooks/use-attempts.ts: exposes insertAttempt; derives per-word stats (attempt count, streak) using mastery helpers
src/hooks/use-pin-gate.ts: thin wrapper around PinContext — exposes isUnlocked, unlock, lock

All hooks call the plain async db/ functions, passing the db handle from use-db.

title: Implement DbContext and PinContext
description:
src/context/db-context.tsx:
- Opens IDBDatabase singleton via openDB from src/db/schema.ts
- Provides db handle + loading/error state to the tree
- All db hooks consume this context

src/context/pin-context.tsx:
- State: isUnlocked: boolean
- unlock(pin: string): Promise<boolean> — calls verifyPin; on success calls setSessionUnlocked(); returns false on wrong PIN
- lock(): calls clearSession(), sets isUnlocked false, navigates to /
- On mount: restores isUnlocked from isSessionUnlocked() so page refresh within 4h doesn't require re-entry
- First-run detection: if loadPinHash() === null, unlock() stores the new PIN as the initial PIN

title: Implement PIN utilities (src/lib/pin.ts)
description:
Pure utility functions for parent PIN gate:
- hashPin(pin: string): Promise<string> — SHA-256 via Web Crypto API with static app salt; returns hex string
- verifyPin(pin: string, storedHash: string): Promise<boolean>
- savePinHash(hash: string): void — localStorage under non-obvious key
- loadPinHash(): string | null
- setSessionUnlocked(): void — sessionStorage timestamp
- isSessionUnlocked(): boolean — checks timestamp < SESSION_UNLOCK_HOURS old
- clearSession(): void

Security posture documented in file: SHA-256 + static salt is intentional — threat model is child discovery, not adversarial cracking.

Must have unit test coverage in src/lib/__tests__/pin.test.ts.

title: Implement revision list algorithm (src/lib/revisionList.ts)
description:
Pure function: buildRevisionList(input: RevisionInput): Word[]

RevisionInput contains: allWords, allWeeks, allAttempts, lastSessionWordIds, currentDate, sessionSize (default 10).

Slot composition:
- 4 slots: current week words — core-heavy early in week (days 1-3), extension-heavy later (days 5-7), mixed mid-week
- 3 slots: previous week words — weighted toward recent weeks; exclude any word seen in last 24 hours
- 3 slots: struggling words — wrong >= 2 times, not mastered (uses isMastered from mastery.ts), sorted most-recently-wrong first

Global rules:
- Never include a word that was in lastSessionWordIds
- If a slot can't be filled (too few eligible words), backfill from remaining eligible words
- Return exactly sessionSize words (or fewer if total word pool is smaller)

Must have 100% unit test coverage in src/lib/__tests__/revisionList.test.ts.

title: Implement mastery helpers (src/lib/mastery.ts)
description:
Pure functions with no side effects:
- consecutiveCorrect(attempts: Attempt[]): number — counts trailing correct streak from most-recent attempt backwards
- isMastered(attempts: Attempt[]): boolean — true when consecutiveCorrect >= MASTERY_THRESHOLD (3)

Answers compared case-insensitively with leading/trailing whitespace trimmed (this comparison lives in session.tsx, but mastery logic lives here).

Must have 100% unit test coverage in src/lib/__tests__/mastery.test.ts.

title: Implement DB access layer (weeks, words, attempts)
description:
Create plain async functions in src/db/ that accept IDBPDatabase as first argument — no hooks, no module-level singletons.

src/db/weeks.ts: getAllWeeks, getWeek, upsertWeek, deleteWeek
src/db/words.ts: getWordsByWeek, getAllWords, upsertWord, deleteWord
src/db/attempts.ts: insertAttempt, getAttemptsForWord, getAttemptsAfter, getAllAttempts, getRecentAttemptsGroupedByWord
src/db/index.ts: re-exports all of the above

All functions fully typed with the types from src/types/index.ts.

title: Implement IndexedDB schema (openDB + store definitions)
description:
Create src/db/schema.ts using idb v8.

Object stores:
- 'weeks': keyPath='id', index by-weekNumber (unique)
- 'words': keyPath='id', index by-weekId
- 'attempts': keyPath='id', indexes: by-wordId, by-date, by-wordId-date (compound)

IDs generated with nanoid() at write time, never in components.
Export a singleton openDB promise used by db-context.

title: Define TypeScript types and constants
description:
Create all shared types and constants:
- src/types/index.ts: Week, Word, Attempt, WordCategory ('core'|'tricky'|'extension'), AppMode
- src/constants/db.ts: DB_NAME='spelling-app', DB_VERSION=1, store name strings
- src/constants/config.ts: SESSION_SIZE=10, MASTERY_THRESHOLD=3, STRUGGLING_MIN_WRONG=2, SESSION_UNLOCK_HOURS=4, TTS_RATE=0.85
- src/constants/strings.ts: ALL user-facing text including button labels, aria-labels, celebration messages, error messages — no magic strings in components

title: Epic: React contexts & custom hooks
description:
Implement the two global contexts and all custom hooks:

Contexts (src/context/):
- db-context.tsx: holds open IDBDatabase singleton, loading/error state
- pin-context.tsx: isUnlocked, unlock(pin), lock() — reads hash from localStorage, stores unlock timestamp in sessionStorage (4h window)

Hooks (src/hooks/):
- use-db.ts: openDB singleton, ready state
- use-weeks.ts, use-words.ts, use-attempts.ts: load/mutate each store
- use-revision-session.ts: fetches all data, calls buildRevisionList, returns session word list (computed once on mount)
- use-tts.ts: Web Speech API wrapper — speak(text), cancel(), isSpeaking, isSupported; cancels before re-speaking (iOS fix); en-GB voice selection with voiceschanged handling
- use-pin-gate.ts: reads auth state from PinContext

Depends on: DB layer (Epic 2) and lib layer (Epic 3).

title: Epic: Core logic library (revision algorithm, mastery, PIN)
description:
Implement all pure-function business logic in src/lib/:
- revisionList.ts: buildRevisionList(input) pure function producing the 10-word session list (4 current-week, 3 previous-weeks, 3 struggling)
- mastery.ts: consecutiveCorrect and isMastered helpers
- pin.ts: SHA-256 PIN hashing via Web Crypto API, sessionStorage helpers

All three modules must have 100% unit test coverage in src/lib/__tests__/.

Depends on: types & constants (Epic 2).

title: Epic: Core types, constants & DB layer
description:
Define all TypeScript types (Week, Word, Attempt, WordCategory), constants (strings.ts, config.ts, db.ts), and the IndexedDB access layer (src/db/schema.ts, weeks.ts, words.ts, attempts.ts).

The DB layer exports plain async functions that accept IDBPDatabase as first arg — no hooks, no singletons — so they are testable without React.

Depends on: project scaffold epic.

title: Tests: migration, profiles DB, child context, and UI
description: Write tests for all new multi-child functionality. (1) src/db/__tests__/migration.test.ts — WRITE FIRST: seed IDB at v1 with attempts lacking childId, trigger upgrade, assert all attempts have childId='child-legacy-1', 'Child 1' profile exists, by-childId index present. (2) src/db/__tests__/profiles.test.ts — CRUD for profiles store. (3) src/context/__tests__/child-context.test.tsx — null initial state, setActiveChild persists, remount re-resolves from IDB. (4) src/pages/child/__tests__/home.test.tsx — picker shown for multi-profile, auto-select for single. Update existing tests: revisionList.test.ts attempt() helper needs childId field; session.test.tsx mock useChild and assert childId in insertAttempt calls.

title: Parent settings: child profile management section
description: Update src/pages/parent/settings.tsx. Add a 'Children' section above 'Session size'. Uses useChildProfile() hook. Section contains: list of current profiles with inline rename (tap name to edit) and delete button; 'Add child' form (text input + save). Delete is blocked if child has attempt records — show 'Cannot delete — has X attempt records' message instead. Add new strings to src/constants/strings.ts: LABEL_WHO_IS_PRACTISING, BTN_ADD_CHILD, LABEL_CHILD_NAME, PLACEHOLDER_CHILD_NAME, MSG_CHILD_NAME_REQUIRED, MSG_CANNOT_DELETE_CHILD(n), GREETING(name).

title: Parent progress page: per-child view with child selector
description: Update src/pages/parent/progress.tsx. Add state: profiles: ChildProfile[] (loaded once on mount), selectedChildId: string (defaults to first profile, controlled by dropdown). Add a select dropdown above the word stat list. The existing useEffect that fetches attempt data gains selectedChildId as a dependency and passes it to the now child-scoped attempt queries. If no profiles exist yet (fresh install), show a message directing parent to add a child in Settings.

title: Session page: null guard for missing active child
description: Update src/pages/child/session.tsx. Add guard at top of component: if activeChild===null, redirect to / immediately. This prevents direct URL navigation to /session with no profile selected. The insertAttempt call site needs no manual change — the hook now injects childId internally (TypeScript will enforce via Omit<Attempt, 'childId'> param type).

title: Import/export word lists as XML
description: Parents need a way to back up their word lists and share them across devices. Add export (download XML) and import (upload XML) functionality to the parent Settings page.

title: Configure vite-plugin-pwa, manifest and service worker
description:
Update vite.config.ts to configure vite-plugin-pwa:
- strategies: 'generateSW'
- registerType: 'autoUpdate'
- workbox.globPatterns: ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,woff2}']
- manifest embedded in plugin config (not separate file unless needed)

public/manifest.json:
- name: 'Spelling Practice', short_name: 'Spelling'
- start_url: '/', display: 'standalone', orientation: 'any'
- background_color: '#ffffff', theme_color: '#6366f1'
- icons: 192x192 and 512x512 (maskable)

public/: add placeholder 192x192 and 512x512 PNG icon files

index.html additions:
- <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-capable' content='yes'>
- <meta name='apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style' content='default'>
- <link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/icon-192.png'>
- viewport: user-scalable=no

Verify: npm run build produces dist/ with sw.js and manifest included.

title: Wire React Router v6 routes (App.tsx + main.tsx)
description:
src/main.tsx:
- Mount React app with DbContext and PinContext providers wrapping BrowserRouter
- StrictMode enabled

src/App.tsx:
- Define all routes using react-router-dom v6 createBrowserRouter or JSX <Routes>:
  - / → pages/child/home.tsx
  - /session → pages/child/session.tsx
  - /summary → pages/child/summary.tsx
  - /parent → <ParentRoute> wrapper with <Outlet />
    - /parent/pin → pin-entry.tsx
    - /parent/dashboard → parent/dashboard.tsx
    - /parent/weeks/new → parent/week-new.tsx
    - /parent/weeks/:weekId → parent/week-detail.tsx
    - /parent/progress → parent/progress.tsx
    - /parent/settings → parent/settings.tsx
- 404 fallback redirects to /

title: Build shared UI primitives (src/components/ui/)
description:
Pure reusable components with no business logic:
- button.tsx: variants (primary, secondary, destructive, ghost); always min-h-14 to meet 56px touch target rule
- badge.tsx: category colours — core=blue, tricky=amber, extension=purple
- card.tsx: standard padded container
- spinner.tsx: loading indicator
- star-rating.tsx: 1-3 star display for session summary

All components use only Tailwind classes, no inline styles except truly dynamic values.
All labels and aria-labels use strings from src/constants/strings.ts.

title: Build parent progress and settings screens
description:
src/pages/parent/progress.tsx:
- Per-word mastery view using attempts data
- Sorted: struggling first (wrong >= 2, not mastered), then in-progress, then mastered
- Simple horizontal CSS bar chart per word (no charting library dependency)
- Uses src/components/parent/progress-chart.tsx

src/pages/parent/settings.tsx:
- Session size control: numeric input, default 10, range 5-20, saved to localStorage
- Change PIN form: verify current PIN first, then enter new PIN twice
- 'Clear all data' destructive button: requires two separate confirmation clicks before executing deleteDatabase()

title: Build parent word management screens (dashboard, week-detail, week-new)
description:
src/pages/parent/dashboard.tsx:
- List of all weeks as cards showing weekNumber, focusSound, word count
- Quick stats: total words, total sessions this week
- '+ New Week' button/FAB
- Link to /parent/progress

src/pages/parent/week-new.tsx:
- Form: weekNumber (number), focusSound (text)
- On submit: upsertWeek(), navigate to /parent/weeks/:weekId

src/pages/parent/week-detail.tsx:
- Words grouped by category (core / tricky / extension) with coloured Badge
- Add word inline form at bottom of each category group
- Edit word: inline edit state on the row
- Delete word: confirmation step (no accidental deletes)
- Uses src/components/parent/word-form.tsx and word-list-item.tsx

title: Build PIN entry component and ParentRoute guard
description:
src/components/parent/pin-entry.tsx:
- 4-digit PIN input (either 4 separate single-digit inputs that auto-advance, or a single masked input)
- Label is lock icon + 'Enter code' — NO text saying 'Admin', 'Parent', or 'Settings'
- On correct PIN: PinContext.unlock() then navigate to /parent/dashboard
- On wrong PIN: shake animation (CSS keyframe), clear all digits, no lockout
- First-run: if no PIN hash stored, prompt 'Choose a 4-digit code' and confirm entry

<ParentRoute> wrapper component (can live in src/components/parent/ or src/App.tsx):
- Reads PinContext.isUnlocked
- If false: <Navigate to='/parent/pin' replace />
- If true: renders <Outlet />

title: Build child session page (session orchestration)
description:
src/pages/child/session.tsx:
- On mount: calls use-revision-session to get the word list (computed once, never re-computed)
- Shows loading spinner while session is being prepared
- Presents one word at a time — NEVER shows the word as visible text at any point during the session
- On mount and on each new word: calls useTts().speak() triggered by a user-gesture proxy (the 'Start'/'Next' button press that advances the word)
- Renders ReplayButton (always visible), SpellingInput, ResultPanel
- Answer comparison: case-insensitive, trimmed
- On submit: calls insertAttempt(wordId, correct), shows ResultPanel
- On 'Next word': advance(); if isComplete navigate to /summary with session stats in router state

title: Build child home and summary pages
description:
src/pages/child/home.tsx:
- Large 'Start Practice' CTA button (full-width, min-h-14)
- If no words exist in any week: friendly message from strings.ts ('Ask a grown-up to add some words') with start button disabled
- Hidden parent entry: 3-second long-press on the app title triggers navigate('/parent/pin'). Implemented via onPointerDown timer + onPointerUp/onPointerMove cancel (cancel if pointer moves >10px). No opacity-0 button — not discoverable by a11y tools.
- Font >= 24px throughout, touch targets >= 56px

src/pages/child/summary.tsx:
- Star rating: 1 star (<50%), 2 stars (50-79%), 3 stars (>=80%) — calculated from session results
- Copy always positive regardless of score (from strings.ts)
- 'Practice again' and 'All done' buttons
- No word-by-word breakdown table

title: Build child session components (SpellingInput, ReplayButton, ResultPanel)
description:
src/components/child/spelling-input.tsx:
- Controlled input, type='text'
- autoCorrect='off' autoCapitalize='off' autoComplete='off' spellCheck={false}
- min font-size 24px, min touch target 56px height
- Submits on Enter key and on 'Check' button press
- Clears between words (value reset on word change)

src/components/child/replay-button.tsx:
- Always visible (never hidden or disabled except while mid-utterance)
- min 56x56px touch target
- Pulse animation while isSpeaking=true
- Calls useTts().speak(currentWord) on press

src/components/child/result-panel.tsx:
- Correct: celebration CSS animation (Tailwind animate-bounce or custom keyframe), positive copy from strings.ts
- Incorrect: shows correct spelling in calm neutral colour (text-slate-700), NO red, NO X mark, NO buzzer
- 'Next word' button to advance
- Word text never appears until result is shown (and even then only on incorrect reveal)

title: Epic: Routing, PWA manifest & service worker
description:
Wire up React Router v6 and configure the app as an installable PWA.

Routing (src/App.tsx + src/main.tsx):
- / → child home (no auth)
- /session, /summary → child session screens
- /parent → <ParentRoute> guard wrapper
- /parent/pin, /parent/dashboard, /parent/weeks/new, /parent/weeks/:weekId, /parent/progress, /parent/settings

PWA:
- vite-plugin-pwa with Workbox generateSW strategy; registerType: autoUpdate
- public/manifest.json: standalone display, orientation: any, 192+512 icons
- iOS meta tags in index.html: apple-mobile-web-app-capable, apple-touch-icon
- viewport: user-scalable=no (intentional for dedicated child tablet)
- Placeholder icon assets (192x192, 512x512)

Depends on: child UI (Epic 5) and parent UI (Epic 6) being sufficiently complete to wire together.

title: Epic: Parent-mode UI (admin screens & components)
description:
Build all parent-facing screens and components. Parent mode is only reachable via PIN — no visible link in child UI.

Pages (src/pages/parent/):
- dashboard.tsx: week list with WeekCard (weekNumber, focusSound, word count), quick stats, '+ New Week' FAB
- week-new.tsx: create week form (weekNumber, focusSound)
- week-detail.tsx: words grouped by category (core/tricky/extension) with badges; add/edit/delete words; confirmation before delete
- progress.tsx: per-word mastery view — struggling first, then in-progress, then mastered; simple CSS bar chart (no charting library)
- settings.tsx: session size (default 10, range 5-20); change PIN form; 'Clear all data' with double-confirmation

Components (src/components/parent/):
- pin-entry.tsx: 4-digit PIN pad; no 'Admin' label — just lock icon + 'Enter code'; shake on wrong PIN; no lockout
- week-form.tsx, word-form.tsx, word-list-item.tsx, progress-chart.tsx

Routing guard: <ParentRoute> wrapper redirects to /parent/pin when PinContext.isUnlocked === false.

Depends on: contexts & hooks (Epic 4).

title: Epic: Child-mode UI (session screens & components)
description:
Build all child-facing screens and components. UX rules that must never be broken:
- Word text NEVER shown during a session (audio only)
- Wrong answer: gentle correct-spelling reveal, no red X, no buzzer
- Touch targets >= 56px, font >= 24px (32px for prompt area)
- autoCorrect/autoCapitalize/spellCheck all disabled on input
- Replay button always visible

Pages (src/pages/child/):
- home.tsx: 'Start Practice' CTA; 3-second long-press on title navigates to /parent/pin (hidden parent entry)
- session.tsx: drives use-revision-session; shows one word at a time via TTS; ReplayButton + SpellingInput + ResultPanel
- summary.tsx: end-of-session stars (1-3 based on %); 'Well done!' copy regardless of score

Components (src/components/child/):
- spelling-input.tsx: controlled input with all autocorrect attrs off
- replay-button.tsx: always visible; pulse while speaking
- result-panel.tsx: correct = celebration animation; incorrect = calm correct-spelling reveal
- session-summary.tsx: star rating display

Depends on: contexts & hooks (Epic 4).

title: Write component tests (session, spelling-input, result-panel, pin-entry)
description:
src/pages/child/__tests__/session.test.tsx:
- THE most critical test: word text NEVER appears as a DOM text node at any point during the session flow
- Verify ReplayButton is always present in the DOM
- Verify SpellingInput has autoCorrect='off', autoCapitalize='off', spellCheck=false
- Verify insertAttempt is called with correct=true/false after submission

src/components/child/__tests__/spelling-input.test.tsx:
- Presence of all autocorrect-off attributes
- Enter key triggers submit callback
- Value clears when wordId prop changes

src/components/child/__tests__/result-panel.test.tsx:
- Correct result: celebration content visible, no red colour classes, no X character
- Incorrect result: correct spelling text visible, no red colour classes

src/components/parent/__tests__/pin-entry.test.tsx:
- Wrong PIN: shake CSS class applied, inputs cleared, unlock NOT called
- Correct PIN: unlock called, navigation to /parent/dashboard triggered

title: Write hook integration tests (use-revision-session, use-tts)
description:
src/hooks/__tests__/use-revision-session.test.tsx:
- Mock idb with in-memory store seeded with test data
- Verify hook returns correct word list structure matching revisionList algorithm output
- Verify list does not change after initial mount (stable mid-session)
- Verify advance() and isComplete behaviour

src/hooks/__tests__/use-tts.test.tsx:
- Mock window.speechSynthesis and SpeechSynthesisUtterance
- Verify isSpeaking transitions: false → true on speak(), → false on onend
- Verify cancel-before-speak: calling speak() while isSpeaking cancels previous utterance
- Verify isSupported=false when window.speechSynthesis is undefined
- Verify cleanup: cancel called on unmount

title: Write unit tests for lib/ (revisionList, mastery, pin)
description:
src/lib/__tests__/revisionList.test.ts — full coverage of buildRevisionList:
- Correct 4/3/3 slot composition under normal conditions
- Core-heavy bias (days 1-3 of week) vs extension-heavy bias (days 5-7)
- 24-hour exclusion for previous-week slot
- Struggling slot: wrong >= 2 times, sorted most-recently-wrong first
- Mastery removal: word with 3 consecutive correct excluded from struggling
- Never-same-word-two-sessions rule (lastSessionWordIds exclusion)
- Backfill when a slot has fewer eligible words than its target count
- Edge cases: no words at all, no previous weeks, all words mastered, sessionSize < 10

src/lib/__tests__/mastery.test.ts:
- consecutiveCorrect with mixed correct/incorrect history
- isMastered threshold boundary (2 correct = not mastered, 3 = mastered)
- Empty attempts array

src/lib/__tests__/pin.test.ts:
- hashPin + verifyPin round-trip
- verifyPin returns false for wrong PIN
- isSessionUnlocked returns false after SESSION_UNLOCK_HOURS have elapsed
- clearSession resets the flag

title: Epic: Test suite (unit, hook integration, component)
description:
Write and pass the full test suite using Vitest + React Testing Library.

Unit tests (src/lib/__tests__/) — 100% coverage target:
- revisionList.test.ts: slot composition (4/3/3), 24h exclusion, mastery removal, never-same-word-twice, core-heavy vs extension-heavy bias, edge cases (too few words, no prior weeks, all mastered)
- mastery.test.ts: consecutiveCorrect, isMastered threshold
- pin.test.ts: hash round-trips, session expiry timing

Hook integration tests (fake idb in-memory):
- use-revision-session.test.tsx
- use-tts.test.tsx: mock window.speechSynthesis, verify isSpeaking state transitions and cancel-before-speak

Component tests (RTL):
- spelling-input.test.tsx: autoCorrect='off', spellCheck={false}, Enter-key submit
- result-panel.test.tsx: correct path = celebration, incorrect path = correct spelling shown with no red styling
- session.test.tsx: word text NEVER appears as a DOM text node during session
- pin-entry.test.tsx: wrong PIN triggers shake animation, correct PIN calls unlock + navigates
